design_philosophy.core_loop: Action-based and menu-driven hybrid battle system within an RPG framework; explore towns, gain experience, acquire items and equipment, engage in combat encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing real-time action reflexes against strategic menu-based RPG decision-making; resource management through item collection and stat progression
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as two young ninja brothers exploring a mysterious invaded fantasy world, uncovering the reason for Chinaland's invasion through adventure and combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Culture Brain's distinctive approach to NES RPGs: hybrid action-menu systems that reject pure turn-based or pure action paradigms. The game recontextualizes its spiritual predecessor Kung-Fu Heroes as an RPG with progression mechanics rather than pure arcade action.

difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based save system allowing progression recovery and testing
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Regional difficulty scaling: US version deliberately increased random encounter rate compared to Japanese version, suggesting developers adjusted challenge for Western audiences. Developer password 'FULL' allows maxed stats, indicating designers anticipated need for difficulty circumvention.

progression.upgrade_system: Experience-based stat progression; equipment acquisition (best equipment obtainable); consumable items collection
progression.unlock_mechanism: Prism Bells system (collectible unlock mechanism); story-gated progression through town exploration and NPC interaction
replayability_hooks: Multiple character sprite/costume variations between regions
Hidden sound test mode accessible via password
Password system enabling non-linear access to content for testing/replay
Sequence breaking potential through developer 'FULL' password

world_building_techniques: NPC dialogue in towns (Chatzy features Journey to the West-inspired NPCs in Japanese version)
Environmental exploration revealing world state (invasion of Chinaland)
Character portrait gallery in ending sequence showing supporting cast
Regional narrative adjustments (removal of drinking scene with Xuan Zang character due to Nintendo content policies)

notable_bosses: Final boss accessible via 'FULL' password suggests endgame progression validation

faction_indicators: Invasion narrative (unnamed antagonistic force invading Chinaland)
Two-brother protagonist dynamic suggesting familial alliance structure
danger_signals: Tiger Tank enemy (originally 'Hittora' with Nazi imagery in Japanese version; heavily censored for US release, indicating sensitivity to militant symbolism)
Masked ninja bandit enemies with unicorn horns suggesting rival combatant factions
environmental_storytelling: Character placement within towns (Journey to the West NPCs in specific town locations)
Enemy variety suggesting organized opposing forces

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Little Ninja Brothers (regional differences, unused content, design choices)
Wikipedia - Little Ninja Brothers (series context, release information, game genre classification)
